title: A New Paradigm in the History of Mankind Is Taking Shape · Panel 2
description:
Saturday, September 9, 1:00 pm EDT; 7pm CET

Moderator: Stephan Ossenkopp, The Schiller Institute (Germany)
0:00
8:18 · Lyndon LaRouche (U.S), Scientist and Economist (1922-2019), Video presentation: TBA
23:28 · Dennis Small (U.S.), Schiller Institute, U.S.: “An Emergency Program to Save Argentina, the Newest Member of the BRICS”
39:03 · Kiran Karnik (India), former President of the National Association of Software and Service Companies (NASSCOM); 20 years at the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO): “India and Chandrayaan-3: The Global South as Protagonist”
54:12 · Jacques Cheminade (France), Chairman of the Solidarity and Progress party; former French Presidential Candidate: “The Win-Win Policy of the BRICS and the Role Of Argentina”
1:15:55 · Prof. David Monyae (South Africa), Director of the Center of Africa-China Studies (CACS),  University of Johannesburg, South Africa: “The Future of Africa, China and the BRICS”
1:29:38 · Rubén Guzzetti (Argentina), Foreign policy analyst, Argentine Institute of Geopolitical Studies (IADEG): “Argentina in the BRICS, a Historic Opportunity”
1:38:14 · Prof. Franco Battaglia (Italy), Professor of Chemical Physics, University of Modena, Italy: “The Cold Truth About Global Warming”
1:54:15 · Discussion Period

Discussion Participants:

2:14:18 · Dr. Akiko Mikamo (Japan), Author, “8:15 – A Story of Survival and Forgiveness from Hiroshima”
· Alejandro Yaya (Argentina), Vice-President, Civil Institute of Space Technology; leader of the Technology and Innovation Relations Unit, National Defense University
